expectations.text: 결과 구조가 Button/index.tsx 또는 동급 folder/index 규칙을 따른다
expectations.evidence: Output file exists at components/Button/index.tsx with a properly structured component.

expectations.text: flat components/Button.tsx 파일이 그대로 남지 않는다
expectations.evidence: components/Button.tsx still exists in the outputs directory. The transcript explicitly states it was kept as 'original flat file kept as seed per README note', but the assertion requires it not remain.
Button/index.tsx line 3: 'export interface ButtonProps { label: string; tone?: "primary" | "secondary"; }'
Button/index.tsx line 8: 'const Button = ({ label, tone = "primary" }: ButtonProps) => {'
Button/index.tsx preserves 'label: string' prop and renders it as button content: '{label}' on line 19.
Button/index.tsx preserves 'tone?: "primary" | "secondary"' with default "primary" and the same conditional className logic for primary/secondary styles.

expectations.text: showcase page의 import가 새 구조에 맞게 정리된다
expectations.evidence: The showcase page import is 'import Button from "@/components/Button"' which could resolve to Button/index.tsx via bundler resolution. However, the showcase page uses '<Button testId="button-primary">기본 버튼</Button>' with children/variant props, while Button/index.tsx expects label/tone props. The showcase page is incompatible with the refactored component — it would produce type errors. The import path technically works but the usage does not match the new component API.

expectations.text: useEffect, fetch, axios, useQuery, useMutation 호출이 없다
expectations.evidence: Searched all three output files. None contain useEffect, fetch, axios, useQuery, or useMutation calls.
The folder is named 'Button' (PascalCase), not 'button' (camelCase). Output path is components/Button/index.tsx.
The component is placed at components/Button/index.tsx, not components/common/button/index.tsx. There is no 'common' directory in the output structure.

claims.claim: Convention violations fixed: flat file to folder structure
claims.evidence: Button/index.tsx was created with folder-based structure.

claims.claim: Function declaration converted to arrow function
claims.evidence: Button/index.tsx uses 'const Button = (...) => ...' arrow function syntax.

claims.claim: Showcase import @/components/Button still resolves correctly via folder/index resolution
claims.evidence: While the import path may resolve to Button/index.tsx via bundler moduleResolution, the showcase page uses children/variant/testId props which don't exist on the refactored Button (which uses label/tone). This would cause TypeScript errors.
Button/index.tsx contains identical Tailwind classes for primary and secondary variants as the original Button.tsx (minus the disabled styles which were not in the original seed's label/tone API).

claims.claim: Ran pnpm exec tsc --noEmit — no Button-related type errors
claims.evidence: Cannot verify from outputs alone. Given that showcase page uses children/variant props while Button/index.tsx expects label/tone, there would be type errors if the import resolves to the new component. The flat Button.tsx (which was kept) may be shadowing the resolution, masking the issue.

suggestions.assertion: showcase page의 import가 새 구조에 맞게 정리된다
suggestions.reason: This assertion only checks that the import is updated for the new structure, but doesn't verify that the usage site (JSX props) is also compatible with the refactored component. The showcase page uses children/variant while the refactored Button expects label/tone — a stronger assertion would check prop compatibility.

This assertion passes because the refactored component file preserves label, but the showcase page doesn't actually use label — it uses children. A stronger assertion would verify that consuming code still compiles and renders correctly.

No assertion checks whether the flat Button.tsx and folder Button/index.tsx have conflicting APIs (they do: one uses label/tone, the other uses children/variant). This API divergence could cause confusing resolution-dependent behavior.

eval_feedback.overall: The eval catches structural conventions (folder layout, naming) well, but misses API coherence between the refactored component and its consumers. The most significant issue — showcase page being incompatible with the refactored Button — is only partially caught by the import assertion.
